message:
fix(trace): use semantic event equality for exact trace equivalence

TraceMonoid::equivalent_exact now compares events using a new
semantically_equal_event function that checks version, kind, and data
fields, rather than comparing event_sort_key tuples. The sort key
intentionally ignores non-semantic envelope fields like sequence numbers
and timestamps for ordering purposes, but this also caused
equivalent_exact to treat events with different payloads as identical
when they shared the same sort key.

This fixes false positives where two traces with structurally identical
causal orderings but different event payloads (e.g., different
CancelReason variants on region_cancelled events) would incorrectly
report as exactly equivalent.

The docstring is updated to clarify that equivalent_exact ignores
envelope fields while still comparing semantic content.

Test added: verifies that region_cancelled events with shutdown vs
timeout CancelReasons are equivalent() (same causal structure) but not
equivalent_exact() (different semantic payloads).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
